vulnerability.title: Disable Ctrl-Alt-Del Reboot Activation
vulnerability.description: By default, SystemD will reboot the system if the Ctrl-Alt-Del key sequence is pressed. To configure the system to ignore the Ctrl-Alt-Del key sequence from the command line instead of rebooting the system, do either of the following: ln -sf /dev/null /etc/systemd/system/ctrl-alt-del.target or systemctl mask ctrl-alt-del.target Do not simply delete the /usr/lib/systemd/system/ctrl-alt-del.service file, as this file may be restored during future system updates.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then if { rpm --quiet -q kernel rpm-ostree bootc && ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet && { [ -f "/run/.containerenv" ] || [ -f "/.containerenv" ]; }; } ; then systemctl disable ctrl-alt-del.target systemctl mask ctrl-alt-del.target else systemctl disable --now ctrl-alt-del.target systemctl mask --now ctrl-alt-del.target fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Disabling Ctrl-Alt-Del via systemd mask only prevents accidental reboot via keyboard shortcut; it does not affect authentication, networking, or critical services and poses no risk of lockout.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for disable_ctrlaltdel_reboot (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_disable_ctrlaltdel_reboot). Could not verify.

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to produce the necessary tool calls to disable Ctrl-Alt-Del reboot activation by masking the target.

We need to:

1. ln -sf /dev/null /etc/systemd/system/ctrl-alt-del.target
OR systemctl mask ctrl-alt-del.target

We must decide which approach. Both achieve same effect: linking the target unit to /dev/null disables it; systemctl mask also masks it (makes it not start). The remediation suggests either.

We need to output tool calls in the specified format: each function call as a separate line? The spec: we have functions: review_plan, run_cmd, read_file, write_file. We need to produce the fix: likely we just need to run a command (ln or systemctl). We don't need to review or read/write files. So we will call run_cmd with appropriate command.

We must output something like:

We need to produce JSON with plan_description and commands array containing exactly one command string to disable Ctrl-Alt-Del reboot activation.

The fix: mask the systemd target ctrl-alt-del.target to prevent reboot on Ctrl-Alt-Del.

Systemd has a target unit that when triggered leads to reboot? Actually systemd has a special unit ctrl-alt-del.target that is triggered by the keyboard shortcut Ctrl-Alt-Del and causes reboot. To disable, we mask that target so it cannot be started.

Thus we need to run: systemctl mask ctrl-alt-del.target

We need to provide plan_description describing that we mask the systemd target ctrl-alt-del.target to disable the reboot activation.

Thus JSON:

{
  "plan_description": "Mask the systemd ctrl-alt-del.target unit to prevent the Ctrl-Alt-Del keyboard shortcut from triggering a system reboot.",
  "commands": [
    "systemctl mask ctrl-alt-del.target"
  ]
}

Make sure valid JSON.
